explicit_requirements.title: AI Receptionist: Inbound Call Handler
explicit_requirements.description: 24/7 system that handles inbound calls with greeting, intent detection (booking/FAQ/escalation), conversation flow, and seamless handoff to human when needed.
acceptance_criteria: Greeting and conversation flow visible in demo
Intent detection works (booking vs FAQ vs escalation)
Conversation is natural and handles interruptions
Warm transfer to human shown when confidence is low

explicit_requirements.title: Appointment Booking
explicit_requirements.description: AI receptionist can process appointment bookings within the inbound call flow.

explicit_requirements.title: FAQ Handling
explicit_requirements.description: AI receptionist can answer frequently asked questions without routing to human.
acceptance_criteria: At least 2-3 realistic healthcare FAQs answered by AI

explicit_requirements.title: SMS Confirmations
explicit_requirements.description: Appointment bookings trigger SMS confirmation (simulated, no real SMS needed).

explicit_requirements.title: AI Outbound Caller
explicit_requirements.description: Automated follow-up and reminder calls to existing clients with dynamic scripts pulled from CRM contact fields.
acceptance_criteria: Outbound call simulator shown in demo
Script dynamically generated from CRM contact data (name, appt date, last visit, etc.)
Demo shows at least 2 different outbound scenarios

explicit_requirements.title: CRM Integration & Call Analytics
explicit_requirements.description: Integration with GoHighLevel-style CRM data and analytics dashboard logging calls, transcripts, booking rate, escalation rate, confidence scores.
acceptance_criteria: Mock CRM contact list visible in demo
Analytics dashboard shows call volume, intent breakdown, booking rate, escalation rate, confidence metrics

nice_to_have.title: Healthcare/Psychiatry/Medical Practice Workflow Familiarity
nice_to_have.description: Demo context and language should reflect healthcare/medical practice conventions.

nice_to_have.title: Automation Orchestration Visualization
nice_to_have.description: Visual representation of n8n/Make.com-style workflow (greeting → intent → booking → webhook → SMS).

nice_to_have.title: Multilingual Support
nice_to_have.description: English primary; additional languages a plus.

michael_constraints: Web app simulation of the phone-answering system (not real telephony integration)
Browser GPU-based AI messaging system (client-side LLM via WebGPU/WebLLM)
All functionality must be demonstrable in a single web app

demo_approach.concept: TeleHealthy Voice Ops Console web app
demo_approach.hero_feature: Browser-GPU (WebLLM/WebGPU) AI receptionist with real inbound conversation and warm transfer
supporting_features: Outbound dialer with CRM-driven dynamic scripting
Analytics dashboard (call volume, intents, booking rate, escalation rate, confidence, transcripts)
supporting_features_fold_in: Webhook/automation visual pipeline (greeting → intent → booking → webhook → SMS)

tech_stack.ai_engine: WebLLM/WebGPU in-browser LLM with scripted fallback
tech_stack.backend: Frontend-only (optional thin Flask blueprint for persisted logs if needed, but not required)
tech_stack.design: Clinical-but-modern healthtech (calm teal/clinical-green, clean whites, warm accent for live state, accessible UI fonts like Inter/DM Sans)

hard_gates: Every explicit requirement (6 items) addressed in cover letter AND demo/deck
Every nice-to-have represented in demo or deck
Demo builds and loads with no console errors on first paint
Demo URL (/demos/telehealthy/) resolves locally; after deploy, /work-samples/telehealthy returns 200
Demo link appears in cover letter and deck
Demo uses cohesive, bespoke clinical-healthtech design (NOT michaelwegter.com look), no em/en dashes
Browser-GPU conversation is genuinely real (WebGPU) with scripted fallback, not just scripted AI
Simulation is labeled honestly so client trusts the demo's integrity

handoff.produced: brief.json with 6 explicit requirements + 3 nice-to-haves, Michael's web-app + browser-GPU constraints, demo concept, hard gates, caps
handoff.decisions: Build FULL frontend-only self-contained app; hero = browser-GPU AI receptionist with WebGPU + scripted fallback; simulate telephony/CRM/SMS with mock data; clinical-healthtech design; label simulation honestly
handoff.next_needs: planner locks design system (palette + fonts), decides hero exactly (which WebLLM model), finalizes feature traceability matrix
handoff.risks: WebGPU/model-download support varies (mandate lazy-load + scripted fallback, no first-paint dependency); keep simulation labeling clear; thin Flask blueprint only if planner wants persisted call logs beyond the demo session
